ISEN 620 Syllabus

Section 600 (59274)

Survey of Optimization

Spring 2026 - College Station

Course Information

End Time: 3:35PM

End Date: 05/05/2026

Meeting Location:  ETB 1013

Credit Hours:  3

Instructor Details

Shixuan Zhang

Email:  shixuan.zhang@tamu.edu

Office Location:  ETB 4031

Phone:  9794582362

Office Hours

Thursdays 3:45-4:45 pm

Catalog Description

Survey of Optimization. (3-0). Credit 3. Theory and numerical methods for

deterministic linear and nonlinear optimization; topics include linear programming,

unconstrained-nonlinear optimization, constrained-nonlinear optimization,

Lagrange and K-K-T conditions, and numerical algorithms. Prerequisite: MATH 304

or MATH 311.

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  MATH 304 or MATH 311.

Course Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

consider real-world problems and determine appropriate optimization

models (linear or nonlinear, continuous, integer, or mixed-integer) to use;

apply simplex methods to solve continuous linear optimization problems and

interpret the solutions using duality theory;

describe main ideas of behind integer and mixed-integer optimization

solution methods, especially their connections to continuous linear

optimization methods;

recognize some basic nonlinear optimization methods and their potential

limitations based on model characteristics (convex versus nonconvex) and

solution goals (local versus global solutions).

Special Course Designation

None

Grading Policy

Grade Decomposition

Tasks

Percentage

Homework Assignments

10%

Midterm Exam

40%

Final Exam

50%

Both exams are closed-book. The tentative date for the midterm is February 24 (in-

class). The final exam is  scheduled  on May 5, 1-3 pm. A 1-page cheat sheet and a

calculator (with no Internet access) are allowed for both exams.

Homework assignments will be given biweekly. Canvas is set up to allow multiple

submissions and only the last submitted assignment will be graded. Late

submission will incur a deduction of 5% per hour. Some homework assignments will

ask students to use computer tools. For these assignments, students need to

submit their source code, together with the computer outputs. The source code

must be done using designated computer programming languages, or those

approved by the instructor.

Final grades will be determined as follows.

Letter Grade Cutoffs

Range

Letter Grade

[90%, 100%]

[80%, 90%)

[70%, 80%)

C

[60%, 70%)

D

[0%, 60%)

F

About regrading, assignment or exam regrading requests will be considered if

received in writing via email, with the original work attached to your instructor or

the grader **no later than one week after the material was returned**. Note that

the instructor and the grader have the right to regrade the entirety, so it is possible

to lose points. Therefore, it is recommended that students should ask for regrading

only there is substantial reason that the grade is given in error.

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

Course Specific Late Work Policy

Late submission for homework assignments is accepted with the reduction in

points (5% per hour). Makeup exams are only available for excused absence

defined in  Student Rule 7 .

Course Schedule

The following course schedule is tentative and subject to change, depending on the

actual progress and student feedback.

Week 1-4: Introduction and linear optimization modeling

Week 5-8: Simplex methods, linear optimization duality, and sensitivity

analysis

Week 9-10: Introduction to integer and mixed-integer optimization methods

Week 11-15: Selected topics in nonlinear optimization
